text.content:
Of Flesh and Wood

A Scenario inspired by Pinocchio (Carlo Collodi)

Safety Keywords: Blood sacrifices / Mutilation / Body Horror

Once upon a time...

The Hunters arrive in Sinalunga, a small town in the Grand Duchy of Tuscany where a gruesome discovery has been made. Not far from the town, the mutilated corpses of ten people were found in an old well, solving almost all of the many disappearances in recent months that have made the area the epicenter of macabre rumors and tales. The Papacy, determined to clarify what happened, fears that there is an ancient esoteric practice behind the killings, one that involves the sacrifice of innocent souls. Once before Sinalunga had been the setting of a terrible story: the noble Azzurra De Baciocchi was accused of witchcraft for sacrificing young women in search of eternal youth, and burned at the stake in 1513. The task of the Hunters will therefore be to understand what lies behind the many disappearances now afflicting Sinalunga.

The story that led...

Two old alchemists, Antonio and Geppetto, have worked on a magic oak tree, hoping to obtain the fantastic ingredients required to uncover the secret of eternal life. They were led to the supernatural discovery by an ancient text in their possession, which revealed the alchemical formula for unending life, the key component being a body made of enchanted wood.

The mystical material would bestow immortality, but to live among men without frightening them it must be covered in flesh. In the course of their experiments, the two alchemists were astonished to discover that the magical oak had a life of its own. Animated by the power of fairy magic, whose only purpose is to bedevil humans, this piece of wood could bring lies to life.

To prove it, the enchanted oak turned into a living, wooden puppet named Carlo, who by his nature could not help but deceive Geppetto and Antonio about the veracity of their theories. Carlo, unable to speak even a single word of truth to the two alchemists, convinced them that the rite they searched for required harnessing a person's soul inside a wooden body. Antonio and Geppetto then began to kidnap the poor and desperate around Sinalunga, torturing and flaying them alive according to Carlo's instructions. After a few weeks, horrified by the situation he unwillingly created, Carlo escaped to the city.

pages.name: The Story at the Arrival of the Hunters

text.content:
The story at the arrival of the Hunters...

After the discovery of the well, Antonio and Geppetto fled the old carpenter's workshop they used as a laboratory and took refuge in Villa Cilesterino, an old abandoned manor house not far from Sinalunga. Here the two alchemists met the spirit of Azzurra, the ancient owner of the villa, who wanders in this world longing for past glories. The gullible pair did not hesitate to tell her the whole story. Azzurra deceived them in turn, animating the mannequins with her powers and convincing Geppetto and Antonio that their research was heading in the right direction. Azzura's spirit now needs to find Carlo and take advantage of the fairy magic that animates him, using it to bring herself back to life.

Carlo escaped from the old carpenter's shop and is now in Sinalunga. Every morning, thanks to the power of his lies, he disguises himself as a real child. Carlo has even begun attending school, where he met Lucino, a loyal and studious child who took a liking to the living puppet. At Villa Cilesterino, Antonio continues his experiments, animating more and more mannequins that he wraps in human flesh to make them look like ordinary people. Azzurra's magic completes the "miracle" by combining flesh and wood, thereby creating servants to do her will. Geppetto, tricked by Azzurra, has instead returned to Sinalunga, where for days he has been desperately trying to track down Carlo and take him back to his mistress.

Main NPCs

Carlo The Living Puppet

OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a puppet animated by fairy magic. The lies I tell become reality for a short time.

Agenda: Living a carefree life without responsibility / I don't want to abandon Sinalunga and my new friend Lucino, but I'm always afraid that Geppetto and Antonio will catch me.

Gift - Lies: Carlo can bring the lies he tells to life by creating a Descriptor that allows him to perform incredible feats. All lies must be centered on him. For example: I can walk through walls or I am invisible to the human eye. The effects of the lies' power are literal. For this reason, Carlo would not be able to go through a closed door and he would be seen by animals. The effect of Lies lasts for 1 Scene or until a new lie is told.

Geppetto Gullible Alchemist Apprentice

OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a poor man who has always been looking for a way to make a living. I learned the basics of alchemy from master Antonio.

Agenda: Pretend to be Carlo's father and find him / Obtain the secret of eternal life, thanks to alchemy.

Gift - Improvised Alchemist: Geppetto can rely on a series of alchemical compounds contained in vials that he hides under a tacky yellow wig. When he throws a vial, roll a die: 1-2 Explosive: The liquid explodes on contact with the air, causing 1 Wound to everyone within thirty feet. Thick black smoke will engulf the whole area; 3-4 Stench: A nauseating odor fills the air giving 1 Penalty to all actions within the area for the rest of the Scene; 5-6 Air Bomb: All subjects in an area of thirty feet are knocked back and fall to the ground. If they hit an obstacle they can suffer 1 Wound. Geppetto is immune to the effects of his alchemical concoctions.

Antonio Ciliegia Gullible Alchemist

OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an old alchemist who has never discovered anything concrete. Despite my skills and my experience, luck is never on my side.

Agenda: Obtaining the secret of eternal life thanks to alchemy.

Gift - Unlucky Alchemist: Antonio can rely on a series of alchemical compounds contained in vials that he hides in his tunic. When using a vial he rolls 1 die. On a 5 or 6 something goes wrong and Antonio suffers 1 Wound due to his proverbial bad luck. 1. Fire: The target and the surrounding area catch fire. All targets involved suffer 1 Wound. If the fire is not extinguished, burning targets suffer 1 Wound per action. 2. Thunder: The target is knocked over thirty feet away and ends up on the ground. If they hit something they will suffer 1 Wound. 3. Glue: an adhesive compound that quickly solidifies in the air. The target will be paralyzed until the compound is removed. The Glue can take 2 Wounds.

Azzurra The Spirit of the Witch who Spurns Death

OL Hard 7 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am the spirit of an old and powerful witch. My powers allow me to summon the souls of the dead and bind them to my will.

Agenda: To come back to life thanks to Carlo's power.

Gift - Ancient Spirit: Azzurra's body is immaterial and can only be damaged by magic. Azzurra can interact with the world by evoking the souls of the dead, choosing between various effects. 1. Poltergeist: Throw up to 3 objects at once. The force with which the objects are thrown is such that they can cause a Wound. 2. Cacophony of the Dead: Frighten everyone with the agonized voices of the damned. Anyone who hears the cacophony must pass a Defense Check or lose 2 Soma points. 3. Possession: Take possession of a target's body and cause them to perform a single action, albeit not a self-destructive one. Azzurra must change the spirits' effect with each action and must use all three before she can use an effect again.

Vincenzo Carabiniere

OL Medium 5 - Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a loyal soldier and I always try to do my best to help others. Given my integrity, my superiors keep me away from battlefields.

Agenda: Find out what lies behind the disappearances in Sinalunga.

Lucino - The Good Friend

OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a dutiful and studious child who can't wait to learn a trade to help my family.

Agenda: Help Carlo become a responsible child / Work to help adults who behave well and follow the law.

Amabile Burromo

OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a skilled business owner and I manage my inn without delegating anything to anyone. I trust myself only.

Agenda: To make profitable deals, without worrying about scruples.

Threats and Minor NPCs

Marionette

OL Easy 3 - Minor NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: We are souls cursed by Azzurra in the wooden bodies of marionettes. We hate all living beings and want to be destroyed to be free again.

Agenda: Attacking every human we meet.

Gift - Boundless Rage: Azzurra's power animates and forces the Marionettes to do evil acts. When a Marionette suffers the last Wound it will immediately spring on the enemy with incredible momentum as Azzurra's power compels them one last time. The Marionette can take a final action with OL Hard 7. This action must be aimed at harming a living being.

Medoro The Old Bear

OL Medium 5 - Minor NPC (4 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a deformed old bear that has lost all its fur. Azzurra treats me like her faithful dog and I am happy to serve her.

Agenda: I have retained most of my bestial strength, despite old age and the passing of time.

Threat - Distrustful Inhabitants

OL Medium 5

Descriptor: We don't want to attract too much attention to Sinalunga, so we're reluctant to talk to foreigners. Nobody here wants to lose what they have gained through years of hard work.

Information from the townspeople (Roll 1d6 or choose):

  1. 1-2: 10 people have been found, but 11 went missing. Nino's body is still missing. He is Mastro Antonio's apprentice carpenter. Antonio is known as Mastro "Cherry" Ciliegia because of his squat and red nose.
  2. 3-4: Villa Cilesterino is haunted by the ghost of the old owner, Azzurra. She was burned at the stake for witchcraft.
  3. 5-6: The high standard of living in Sinalunga led to the opening of Villa Balocchi, a tavern on the edge of town where one can find company as well as a comfortable bed. The tavern is managed by Amabile Burromo and is in open competition with the historic town inn, the Gambero Rosso (meaning "the Red Prawn").

Key Locations

Sinalunga

Descriptor: A small town in the Tuscan hills. Thanks to its famously delicious wine, many in the town manage to have a good standard of living.

By wandering around town and talking to the inhabitants, the Hunters can get some information. The fear that an intervention by the Grand Duchy could upset life in Sinalunga means the inhabitants are reluctant to speak to anyone other than the authorities. Only Lapo and Cino, who are not particularly bright, won't hesitate to talk with the Hunters, though only with the promise of a reward.

The Old Well

Descriptor: An old abandoned well in the middle of a dilapidated vineyard. A nauseating smell from the bottom of the well reminds anyone who approaches of the horror that it hides.

Information:

  • The bodies have already been removed and burned to avoid infection and disease.
  • When going down into the well, however, there are still some remains and traces of blood.
  • On the bottom, it is possible to find a carpenter's chisel that Geppetto lost while moving the corpses.

The Gambero Rosso Inn

Descriptor: A small and cozy tavern, full of bottles of good wine.

Information:

  • Speaking with the innkeeper, a big fat man named Duccio, the Hunters will learn that Villa Balocchi is a place steeped in sin, where men and women gamble and entertain themselves in shady encounters.
  • During the evening, Geppetto is at the Gambero Rosso and will not hesitate to play out his little rehearsed spectacle of the worried father for whoever shows up. Geppetto will describe Carlo as an annoying child with a pair of old, elegant shoes that Geppetto gave him for his first day of school.

The Old Carpenter's Shop

Descriptor: Once the alchemical laboratory of Mastro Antonio, the carpenter's shop was abandoned after the discovery of the corpses.

Information:

  • The place is a normal carpenter's shop, abandoned for some time. The only element that catches the Hunters' attention is a series of chisels on the wall, with one missing from the set. If the Hunters have recovered the chisel in the well they will see that it is the missing tool.
  • Among other things of interest, the Hunters can find an old leather bag with some books for the local school.
  • At the back of the carpenter's shop are the remains of a hastily packed alchemical laboratory. On the wall, there are two human-sized wooden mannequins, skillfully made but stained with blood and decomposing skin. These are Marionettes, the first two produced by Antonio and Geppetto's experiments, later animated by Azzurra's magic. The Marionettes will spring on the Hunters as soon as they get close.

The School

Descriptor: A small school for the studious children of Sinalunga. Jolly voices and shouts cover the lamentation of a dispirited teacher.

Information:

  • The school is open in the morning and brings together all the town's children who want to learn something. The situation is clearly out of control and the teacher, a young academic from Florence, does not know what to do.
  • The school is attended by Carlo who, however, appears as an ordinary child. In fact, he does not correspond to the description that Geppetto may have given. The old shoes on his feet are the only clue that the Hunters may notice.

Villa Balocchi

Descriptor: A beautiful and sumptuous inn, recently refurbished.

Information:

  • Amabile hosts little Carlo, making him sleep in a small back room.
  • The Puppet pays the greedy innkeeper with stones which he temporarily turns into gold coins with his powers. Throwing the coins into a safe in his room, the innkeeper hasn't yet noticed the Puppet's trick.
  • During the afternoon and evening, Carlo is at Villa Balocchi and can be found playing with Lucino or pretending to study.

Villa Cilesterino

Descriptor: A large manor house, once beautiful but now abandoned to fur.

Information:

  • Villa Cilesterino is divided into 3 environments: the Gardens, the Villa, and the Secret Laboratory.
  • Azzurra is connected to the whole place and will be aware of anyone who ventures onto her property.
  • In the dining room, there is a huge painting on the north wall depicting a large abandoned garden with flowers and plants of all kinds. By looking closely behind the painting, the Hunters can activate the mechanism which opens the secret door to the Secret Laboratory.

The Gardens

Descriptor: A large abandoned garden with plants and flowers overgrown.

Information:

  • The Garden is guarded by 4 Marionettes and old Medoro roam around, a sick bear that Azzurra keeps in her orders. Wandering around the Garden will immediately alert the guardians.

The Secret Laboratory

Descriptor: The cellar of Villa Cilesterino, converted into an alchemical laboratory by Antonio. The smell of wine and mold is unbearable.

Information:

  • Antonio continues his research under Azzurra's guidance. This led him to create a magical brazier in which Carlo is to be burned.
  • Nino, the carpenter's apprentice, is tied to a table, alive but with his legs now ruined by Antonio's slow flaying. Azzurra wants him kept alive, as she needs innocent blood for the rite.

Scenario Events

In this Scenario, there are six Events. As a Storyteller, feel free to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.

  • If the Hunters put pressure on Carlo: Lucino will intervene to help his friend by giving him time to escape.
  • If the Hunters reveal their identity to Vincenzo: The officer will be at their disposal, trying to help them whenever possible.
  • If the Hunters meet Geppetto: The man will attempt to play on their sympathies with the made-up story of the lost son. Geppetto is not a genius, but neither is he stupid. If he understands who the Hunters really are, he will try to follow them to see if they find Carlo.
  • If the Hunters gain Carlo's trust: The Puppet will reveal the truth. Geppetto and Antonio are to blame for everything. The Puppet ignores that it is now Azzurra who runs things.
  • If Geppetto is captured by the Hunters: He will lead them to Villa Cilesterino, where his companion is. Geppetto will pretend to know nothing, aware that once they arrive, Azzurra will get rid of the Hunters.
  • When Azzurra meets the Hunters: She will try to deceive them by pretending to be a good spirit who tries to watch over the inhabitants of Sinalunga. Azzurra will ask the Hunters to help her recover a magical Puppet, animated by a dark force that is devouring the souls of the poor townsfolk.

Exclusive Scenario Gifts

These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Flesh and Wood Scenario and can be acquired once the conditions are met. A Hunter can only acquire one Scenario Gift. If they buy a new one during an Interlude, they will have to forgo the previous one.

Marionettes' Destroyer

Requirement: Defeat any Marionette or understand that they are souls imprisoned against their will.

You know how to calm restless spirits by giving them a proper greeting when their soul is freed. You are not affected by the Boundless Rage Gift. Receive 1 Additional Success when defending against beings that are animated or controlled by spirits or magic.

A True Friend

Requirement: Gain Carlo's trust despite all his lies.

Carlo understands that he can trust you and will run to your aid if you need it. You can spend 1 Soma to have Carlo appear in the Scene as your NPC ally. Carlo will use the power of his lies to help you to the best of his ability.

The Field of Miracles (Special Gift for The Fox and the Cat)

Requirement: Arrive in Sinalunga.

You know Sinalunga well because it is where one of your secret shelters is located. Once per Scene you can spend 1 Soma so you or another subject can leave the Scene and hide in the Field of Miracles. No one can find the exact location of the shelter. When you are at the Field, once per Session you can recover 1 Wound you have suffered.

FIRST SCENE - Arrival In Sinalunga

Read aloud to the Hunters: You have recently arrived in Sinalunga. The small town is bustling with activity and no one seems to have noticed your presence. However, in the eyes of the city's inhabitants you clearly see a darkness, reflecting the horror that has recently been discovered... You notice a man in military uniform, intent on talking to a couple of peasants who are dragging a large pig with an injured leg. The two townspeople look puzzled at the officer, and from their disinterested expressions, you understand that they can't wait to get back on the road and leave the insistent investigator behind. The Order is in close contact with the Grand Duchy of Tuscany, so the officer should be aware that a group of Hunters is arriving in town. Could he be an ally in your investigation?

Place: The road that crosses the whole town of Sinalunga. The townspeople are hard at work, trying to finish all their chores before dinner.

Time: It is less than an hour before sunset and the low, intensely red sun illuminates the surrounding hills, full of vineyards.

Initial situation: The Hunters have arrived in the town of Sinalunga. They already have various pieces of information regarding the incident and they know where the old well, and makeshift morgue, is located. In front of them, Vincenzo Carabiniere, the military officer, is also at work to unravel the murder mystery.

Information - What the Hunters already know:

  • The well is a ten-minute walk from the town and was part of an old manor estate called Villa Cilesterino.
  • The bodies found had their skin flayed off. It seems that the killer wanted only the skin and muscles of their victims, but not the organs and entrails.

Speaking with Vincenzo, the Hunters will discover that the two farmers he was questioning are Lapo and Cino, the ones who found the well. The two were reluctant to talk because at the time of their discovery they were hiding at the well to eat some bread they had stolen from the bakery. The two know very little and Vincenzo will make it clear that they are not the sharpest knives in the drawer.

End of Scene: The Hunters leave in the direction of the old well to better understand what happened. If they want, Vincenzo will accompany them. Go to SECOND SCENE - The Old Well.

SECOND SCENE - The Old Well

Read aloud to the Hunters: You find the old well after a ten-minute walk from the town. On the nearby hill, the old, shabby silhouette of Villa Cilesterino looms over you like a dark shadow. The boards that closed the well are uprooted and are now just laying across the top. The stench of death still lingers in the air, still strong enough that it cannot be ignored.

Location: The old well on the edge of the Cilesterino estate.

Time: The sun is now setting and it won't be light for much longer.

Information:

  • By descending into the well, the Hunters will find Geppetto's chisel.
  • Around the well there are traces of people which then lead towards the abandoned villa.

End of Scene: The Hunters can decide to investigate the Old Carpenter's Shop from which the chisel surely comes (THIRD SCENE OPTION A - At The Old Carpenter's Shop) or follow the tracks to Villa Cilesterino (THIRD SCENE OPTION B - Villa Cilesterino).

THIRD SCENE OPTION A - At The Old Carpenter's Shop

Read aloud to the Hunters: From the outside, the workshop seems abandoned. A large sign outside sways, blown by the wind. It reads "Mastro Antonio Ciliegia, Carpenter." Apparently what they told you in the town is true. It seems that Antonio has left and closed up his business.

Place: Mastro Antonio's carpenter's shop, a well-kept place that, on the inside, does not seem abandoned at all.

Time: It is night now and no inhabitants of Sinalunga wander the streets. The shutters in the windows flutter as prying eyes stare at the Hunters.

Information:

  • Upon entering the Old Carpenter's Shop, the Hunters will come across the Marionettes on guard. Once the clash is resolved, the Hunters will be able to verify that the chisel found in the well is just what is missing from the wall rack.
  • A school bag full of old books attracts their attention. Among other things, they find an unsent letter requesting enrollment for a boy named Carlo.

End of Scene: Hunters can go to Villa Cilesterino (THIRD SCENE OPTION B - Villa Cilesterino) or wait for the morning to go to FOURTH SCENE - The School.

THIRD SCENE OPTION B - Villa Cilesterino

Read aloud to the Hunters: As soon as you step into the large garden of the abandoned villa you realize that what was once a magnificent garden is now nothing more than a thick mass of scrub and weathered stone statues. Your attention, however, is attracted by a bestial verse, which materializes in the grotesque figure of a bear, but one without fur, the hairless skin pocked by who knows what disease. The creature sniffs the air. After spotting you, it starts charging.

Time: It is night and a cloud-veiled moon illuminates the old marble statues in the garden with a gloomy glow.

Information:

  • Medoro lashes out at the Hunters. Azzurra will intervene at the end of the battle to ask the Hunters to spare the unfortunate creature she uses as a guardian. Then, she will show them the Marionettes in the garden, saying that in the town someone is carrying out atrocious experiments on the townsfolk. Azzurra will tell the Hunters that everything started with a small puppet animated by dark magic. Azzurra will ask for the Hunters' help. They should bring back the puppet who hides among the townspeople pretending to be a real child named Carlo. Once captured and brought before her, Azzurra will use her power to banish the evil spirit that animates him.
  • Azzurra's intention is to deceive the Hunters, in the hope that they will bring her little Carlo.

End of Scene: Hunters can also go to the Old Carpenter's Shop in search of clues (THIRD SCENE OPTION A - At The Old Carpenter's Shop) or wait for the morning to go to FOURTH SCENE - The School if they have already obtained the clues from the carpentry.

FOURTH SCENE - The School

Read aloud to the Hunters: The small school of Sinalunga welcomes no more than thirty students. A young teacher with the typical Florentine accent yells and gestures in a vain attempt to get the unruly young scholars to settle down. But children run everywhere like ferrets, ignoring the disconsolate man's calls.

Information:

  • Watching the children play, the Hunters will notice that one of them has no school bag or books with him. If asked about it, the teacher will say that the pupil is called Carlo and that he is an orphan who comes to school but has never been officially enrolled. The man is not sure where he lives and what he does outside of school.
  • If a Hunter knows magic, they will sense that there is something strange about the child.
  • Carlo, sure of his disguise, plays with his friend Lucino, but as soon as the Hunters corner him he will try to escape using the power of his lies.
  • Once captured, Carlo will reveal the whole truth to the Hunters. The Puppet doesn't know about Azzurra but will speak of Geppetto and Antonio as two unscrupulous killers.

End of Scene: The Hunters have managed to capture Carlo. Whether they decide to believe him or not, it is clear that Villa Cilesterino is hiding a secret. Go to the FIFTH SCENE - Return to the Villa.

FIFTH SCENE - Return to the Villa

Read aloud to the Hunters: When you arrive at the Villa you perceive a strange tension in the air. The weeds that had swallowed the garden are uprooted as if displaced by a supernatural force that has created a path for you. The door of the mansion is wide open, while from the broken windows, the dim light of the candelabra suggests that some kind of welcome is awaiting you.

Location: Villa Cilesterino and the Secret Laboratory in the basement, where Antonio and Geppetto carry out their grim experiments.

Time: It is just past noon, but the sky is full of clouds, giving a gloomy atmosphere to the abandoned villa.

Information: At this point, the story can go in two distinct directions.

  • If the Hunters trust Azzurra: The spirit will hand over Carlo to Antonio and Geppetto. The two will take refuge in the basement dragging the weeping puppet with them. At this point, Azzurra will order the Hunters to leave. If not listened to, she will try to get rid of them with the help of the Marionettes and Medoro, if it's still alive.
  • If the Hunters believe Carlo: Azzurra will try to kill them in order to capture the puppet but will not hesitate to flee if the situation turns for the worse. Antonio and Geppetto, abandoned by their mistress, will take refuge in the Secret Laboratory, trying to put up a last resistance to the Hunters.

End of Scene: However it goes, the Hunters will have Carlo's fate in their hands and somehow, they will shed light on the disappearances that took place in Sinalunga.

flags.setting: Sinalunga, Grand Duchy of Tuscany, Italy
